bull.b: AWS is a durable, high‑margin earnings engine: accelerated 28% growth to a ~$150B run rate, with AI services (Bedrock) and custom silicon adoption driving sustained enterprise spend and operating income contribution over coming quarters.

Advertising and marketplace services leverage Amazon’s shopping intent and merchant base, generating recurring, high‑margin fee revenue. Ads growth (22% YoY) and marketplace liquidity create a durable, diversified revenue stream less tied to first‑party retail cycles.

bull.t: Stronger Balance Sheet And Funding Access
bull.b: Improved leverage metrics plus demonstrated access to deep capital markets (multi‑tranche bond deals and a $17.5B facility) provide durable financial flexibility to fund AWS/AI and logistics investments while managing refinancing timelines and strategic initiatives.

bear.t: Heavy Capex Pressuring Free Cash Flow
bear.b: Very large, sustained CapEx—primarily for AWS and generative AI capacity—compresses free cash flow until new capacity is monetized. Negative TTM FCF reduces near‑term financial flexibility and raises the payback horizon on recent investments.

Chip and component constraints (Trainium sold‑out, memory/storage inflation) create structural bottlenecks for monetizing AI and cloud demand. Allocation decisions and constrained supply can delay revenue realization and inflate near‑term unit economics.

While leverage ratios improved, absolute debt levels have increased via large issuances. Higher nominal debt amplifies refinancing and rate risks, potentially increasing interest costs and constraining capital allocation if rates or funding conditions shift.
